title: Cephaelis debeauxii
intro: Cephaelis debeauxii, also known as Beaux's coffee or Beaux's tea, is a plant species in the family Rubiaceae, native to Central and South America. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions due to its reported health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve digestion. The bioactive constituents of Cephaelis debeauxii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often consumed to promote relaxation, improve sleep quality, and alleviate symptoms of anxiety and stress.
taxonomy: Cephaelis debeauxii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Central America, specifically in Panama. The classification of Cephaelis debeauxii is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. This plant is often found in humid and subtropical forests where it can thrive under shaded conditions. More research is required to confirm its exact classification and other details about its growth habits.
